name: SPINAL CORD
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
definition: space-occupying CSF-isointense intradural / intraspinal extramedullary lesion with EXTRADURAL LESIONS OF SPINE

demographics.Prevalence: 4-10% of all CNS tumors; 20% of all intraspinal tumors in adults (35% in
demographics.Histo: metastases spread from marrow via venous foramina into spinal canal

discriminating_features: variable degrees of enhancement (vast majority with some enhancement)

general: variable degrees of enhancement (vast majority with some enhancement)
involves half the cross-sectional area of cord
extends to anterior two-thirds of cord

frequently central convex appearance (= disruption of posterior vertebral cortex by
frequently central convex appearance (= disruption of posterior vertebral cortex by
